name: National Science Museum, Daejeon
concept&appearance: The museum is designed as a large-scale campus complex situated in the Daedeok Science Complex, themed around the 'harmonious development of nature and human beings'. The architecture features a collection of distinct buildings rather than a single monolithic structure, including the Science & Technology Hall, Natural History Hall, and a prominent dome-shaped Planetarium which is the largest in Korea. The layout integrates with the surrounding environment, featuring outdoor exhibition spaces and nature trails.
permanent_exhibitions: The museum houses several major exhibition halls: 1. Science & Technology Hall: The main hall featuring exhibits on the Industrial Revolution, Future Life, Basic Science, Chemistry, and Life Science, showcasing the history and future of Korean and global technology. 2. Natural History Hall: Displays fossils, minerals, and dioramas (including dinosaur models) to explain the history of the Earth and biodiversity. 3. Human Evolution Hall: Dedicated to the evolution of mankind from early hominids to modern humans. 4. Science Alive Discovery Center: An interactive hall with hands-on exhibits on floors 1-3 designed to engage visitors with scientific principles. 5. Future Tech Hall: Focuses on emerging technologies and the 4th Industrial Revolution. 6. KkumAty Hall: A dedicated space for children's science education. 7. Outdoor Exhibits: Features large-scale models including aircraft, a steam engine, and a replica of Cheomseongdae (ancient observatory).
central_hall: The complex is centered around the extensive outdoor grounds and the Science & Technology Hall. The Astronomical Hall, featuring a massive dome, serves as a central landmark of the campus. The interior of the main halls is described as having a functional, cavernous layout with exhibition areas partitioned for diverse scientific themes.
special_effects_theaters: 1. Planetarium: Features a 23-meter dome screen (the largest in Korea) with a seating capacity of approximately 208-242 people, capable of projecting realistic night skies and astronomical phenomena. 2. Astro-vision Theater: Equipped with a 70mm projection system for large-format science films.
science_popularization_activities: The museum runs comprehensive educational programs including the 'Science Education Center' which offers classes on topics like HAM radio, model plane construction, and computer science. It also hosts science camps, cultural events, and the 'Science Alive' programs to foster scientific creativity among youth.
